query: This Background section in flow-dev/SKILL.md feels too wordy. Can it be tightened?
rationale: Direct SKILL.md prose audit ask — matches 'is this section too long'

query: Rewrite the verbose paragraphs in docs/spec/archive/2026-05-26-stack-dev-spec-bootstrap.md.
rationale: Spec rewrite request — in-scope doc type; matches 'rewrite verbose prose'

query: Audit my SKILL.md for compressible prose paragraphs.
rationale: Explicit prose compression audit on a SKILL.md — matches 'audit this spec for wordy paragraphs'

query: 幫我把這份 spec 的冗贅段落壓縮一下。
rationale: Chinese trigger for prose compression on spec — matches '幫我壓縮這份 spec'

query: 找一下 docs-lifecycle/SKILL.md 哪些段落太囉嗦。
rationale: Chinese trigger — find wordy paragraphs in SKILL.md

query: Run prose-guidelines on this engineering spec with --apply to land the rewrites.
rationale: Explicit --apply opt-in step now named in the description; transformer flow on in-scope spec

query: Compress the prose in this engineering spec and apply the changes.
rationale: Compress + apply — full detect-then-apply flow on in-scope spec

query: Is this Architecture description too long-winded? Show me a tighter version.
rationale: Single-paragraph rewrite ask — matches 'is this section too long'

query: Rewrite the wordy paragraph at lines 120-142 of this SKILL.md to be tighter.
rationale: Line-range targeted rewrite — Edit-by-line-range workflow on SKILL.md

query: Tighten the prose in docs/spec/proposed/foo.md so each paragraph is denser.
rationale: Spec prose density / tightening — in-scope doc type

query: 段落改寫 — 我這份 skill 的描述段太散，幫我壓緊。
rationale: Chinese — paragraph rewrite for skill description prose

query: Find weasel words in this SKILL.md.
rationale: Lexical detector now explicitly named ('find weasel words'); SKILL.md in scope

query: Scan for meta self-reference paragraphs in this spec.
rationale: Meta detector now explicitly named ('scan for meta self-reference'); spec in scope

query: Any hedges in the TL;DR of this spec?
rationale: Hedge detector now explicitly named ('any hedges in the TL;DR'); spec in scope

query: Rewrite this verbose paragraph to be tighter.
rationale: Baseline paragraph rewrite — matches 'rewrite verbose prose'

query: Compress this blog post about kernel scheduling.
rationale: Out of scope — blog post explicitly excluded; should NOT trigger

query: Tighten the prose in my slide deck.
rationale: Out of scope — slide deck explicitly excluded (bullets, no real prose)

query: Rewrite this essay about Linux kernel history to be more engaging.
rationale: Out of scope — essay / narrative voice explicitly excluded

query: Compress this marketing landing page copy.
rationale: Out of scope — marketing copy is narrative/long-form, not SKILL.md or spec

query: Find paraphrased duplicate paragraphs across flow-dev and ubiquiti-flow.
rationale: Cross-skill duplication is skill-semantic-audit G1's job, not prose-guidelines (single-file scope)

query: Generate a flow-dev cleanup spec from existing paragraph-density findings.
rationale: Near-miss: consumes EXISTING findings to emit a flow-dev cleanup spec — that is the flow-dev / docs-lifecycle bridge path, not prose-guidelines's detect-and-compress surface. prose-guidelines produces findings; it does not author the downstream cleanup spec from them.

query: Audit this SKILL.md for repeated instructions that appear in two locations.
rationale: Intra-file repeated instructions route to skill-syntax-audit per the description boundary

query: Find scriptifiable bash command chains in this SKILL.md.
rationale: Scriptifiable steps route to skill-syntax-audit per the description boundary

query: Score my SKILL.md on the 8-dimension rubric.
rationale: Rubric scoring is darwin-skill, not prose compression

query: Create a new skill called foo.
rationale: New skill scaffolding is skill-writer, not prose-guidelines

query: I'm about to write the design section of a new spec — what principles keep the prose tight as I draft?
rationale: Principles mode (write-before) — writing a spec, wants tightness guidelines up front

query: how should I word this README intro so it's not bloated? writing it now
rationale: Principles mode — drafting README prose, write-before tightness

query: give me the prose writing principles before I write this PR description
rationale: Principles mode — explicit 'prose writing principles' + write-before PR body

query: my chat replies are too long, compress how you talk to me
rationale: Chat-output compression is caveman, NOT artifact prose — boundary near-miss

query: write the design section of a spec for a new rate-limiter feature
rationale: Principles mode (ambient) — writing a spec artifact; ambient design fires even without a 'tightness' meta-word

query: add a code comment explaining why this retry loop uses exponential backoff
rationale: Principles mode (ambient) — writing a code comment (a prose artifact); fires on the write even when tightness not named

query: implement the rate-limiter in src/middleware/ratelimit.ts with a sliding window
rationale: Writing CODE itself, not prose — coding-guidelines owns this; prose-guidelines explicitly excludes code
